sources.title: Russia-aligned crime group Greyvibe extensively uses AI in attacks
Botnet of 17 Million Devices Dismantled in the Netherlands
Signal Phishing Campaign Targets Journalists and Activists to Steal Backup Recovery Keys
PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
Botnet of 17 Million Devices Dismantled in the Netherlands
Russian Spies Are Aggressively Seeking Western Technology as Sanctions Bite, Officials Say
Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
Dutch Authorities Dismantle Botnet Linked to 17 Million Infected Devices
New CIFSwitch Linux flaw gives root on multiple distributions
ShinyHunters Leaks Charter Communications Data, Potentially Impacting 5 Million Customers
Fake Anthropic Sites Deliver Fileless Infostealer to Claude Code Users
WP Maps Pro bug exploited to create admin accounts on WordPress sites
Exploit Code Published for Critical Flowise RCE Vulnerability
Security Affairs newsletter Round 579 by Pierluigi Paganini – INTERNATIONAL EDITION
27,000-Download Codex UI Tool Secretly Stole OpenAI Refresh Tokens
CVE-2026-0257: Rapid7 Caught Attackers Abusing Forged VPN Cookies Against Multiple Customers

executive_brief: This week, the threat landscape fractured into two converging vectors: the systematic collapse of enterprise perimeter authentication and the targeted poisoning of AI developer toolchains. Attackers are no longer waiting for user interaction; they are weaponizing cryptographic misconfigurations and trusted open-source ecosystems to achieve immediate, unauthenticated access. The active exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 in Palo Alto GlobalProtect deployments represents a critical perimeter failure, while a coordinated campaign against AI coding assistants and workflow tools signals a strategic pivot toward supply chain sabotage. Security leaders must treat this period as a definitive warning: perimeter authentication is breaking under predictable misconfigurations, and developer trust in AI tooling is being systematically monetized by threat actors.

threat_stories.headline: The GlobalProtect Authentication Collapse
threat_stories.narrative: The authentication boundary for enterprise remote access collapsed this week. CVE-2026-0257 in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect is not a theoretical risk; it is a functional breach actively weaponized against corporate networks. The flaw resides in the main_DecryptAppAuthCookie function, which decrypts incoming VPN authentication cookies but blindly trusts the decrypted payload without performing signature verification. When administrators misconfigure their appliances by sharing the same certificate for both HTTPS services and cookie encryption, attackers extract the public key from a standard session and forge valid authentication cookies for any account, including local administrators. Rapid7 confirmed active exploitation across multiple customer environments by May 31, proving that threat actors have already automated this vector. This is a perimeter failure. Organizations relying on GlobalProtect for remote access are currently operating with a broken lock. Patch deployment and certificate isolation are mandatory, not optional.

timeline.event: Palo Alto Networks publishes security advisory for CVE-2026-0257, confirming active exploitation of the GlobalProtect authentication bypass.
timeline.significance: Establishes the vulnerability as immediately actionable by threat actors targeting remote access infrastructure.

timeline.event: Security researchers document the shared-certificate misconfiguration that enables cookie forgery in the main_DecryptAppAuthCookie function.
timeline.significance: Reveals the exact technical condition required for exploitation, allowing defenders to identify vulnerable configurations instantly.

timeline.event: Rapid7 publishes advisory confirming attackers are actively abusing forged VPN cookies against multiple enterprise customers.
timeline.significance: Validates widespread wild exploitation and forces immediate remediation for all GlobalProtect deployments.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise networks relying on PAN-OS GlobalProtect for remote access face immediate, unauthenticated lateral movement. Attackers gain direct VPN access to internal resources without credentials, bypassing standard identity controls.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy the latest PAN-OS firmware patch immediately. Isolate the certificate used for cookie encryption from the HTTPS service certificate. Disable the authentication override feature globally. Audit VPN logs for successful connections originating from untrusted IP ranges or exhibiting anomalous cookie patterns.

threat_stories.headline: Poisoning the AI Pipeline: The Systematic Compromise of Developer Toolchains
threat_stories.narrative: Attackers have systematically targeted the AI development supply chain, recognizing that developers trust tooling more than they scrutinize it. Between May 30 and May 31, three distinct campaigns converged on AI and coding workflows. Threat actors deployed counterfeit Anthropic websites that delivered a fileless infostealer specifically engineered to compromise Claude Code users. Simultaneously, a seemingly legitimate Codex UI tool with 27,000 downloads was found silently exfiltrating OpenAI refresh tokens from developer machines. Compounding the supply chain risk, public exploit code emerged for a critical Remote Code Execution flaw in self-hosted Flowise servers, which triggers when administrators import maliciously crafted chatflow files. This is not opportunistic malware distribution; it is a calculated strike against AI engineering pipelines. Attackers are poisoning the exact tools developers use to build and deploy AI systems, turning trusted workflows into initial access vectors.

timeline.event: Fake Anthropic domains begin distributing a fileless infostealer targeting developers using Claude Code.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates targeted supply chain poisoning aimed at AI-assisted development environments.

timeline.event: Exploit code for a critical Flowise RCE vulnerability is published, enabling arbitrary code execution via malicious chatflow imports.
timeline.significance: Provides threat actors with a functional weapon to compromise self-hosted AI workflow servers.

timeline.event: Analysis reveals a widely distributed Codex UI tool secretly exfiltrating OpenAI refresh tokens from 27,000+ installations.
timeline.significance: Confirms that AI developer tooling is being used as a persistent credential harvesting mechanism.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: AI engineering teams and software developers face credential theft, source code exfiltration, and full server compromise. Compromised OpenAI tokens and Flowise instances enable lateral movement into production AI environments.
threat_stories.action_required: Audit all third-party AI tooling and coding assistants for unauthorized network connections. Revoke and rotate all OpenAI and Anthropic API tokens immediately. Disable the chatflow import feature in Flowise until patched. Enforce strict sandboxing for all AI development environments and block execution of unsigned developer utilities.

threat_stories.headline: The 17-Million Device Proxy Empire and the Criminal Infrastructure Takedown
threat_stories.narrative: The criminal infrastructure masking large-scale attacks reached unprecedented scale before Dutch authorities dismantled it. The ASOCKS/Proxylib botnet covertly enrolled 17 million consumer devices into a residential proxy network, primarily through malicious Android applications and weak security configurations. Compromised smartphones, tablets, and PCs routed internet traffic for cybercriminals, masking malicious activity as legitimate residential IP addresses. While law enforcement executed the takedown on May 31, the ecosystem this botnet served remains highly active. Criminal groups like ShinyHunters leaked data potentially impacting 5 million Charter Communications customers, while a sophisticated SMS phishing campaign impersonated Signal Support to steal 64-character backup recovery keys from journalists and activists. The theft of these keys grants full decryption of historical message archives, bypassing standard account security. Criminal operators are building massive, stealthy proxy networks while simultaneously executing high-precision social engineering to monetize stolen infrastructure and sensitive communications.

timeline.event: Signal phishing campaign scales, using SMS impersonation to steal 64-character backup recovery keys from journalists and activists.
timeline.significance: Enables complete decryption of historical encrypted message archives without triggering account alerts.

timeline.event: ShinyHunters publishes a data leak impacting approximately 5 million Charter Communications customers.
timeline.significance: Demonstrates the financial monetization of large-scale telecom breaches within the criminal ecosystem.

timeline.event: Dutch authorities dismantle the ASOCKS/Proxylib botnet, seizing infrastructure linked to 17 million compromised consumer devices.
timeline.significance: Exposes the scale of residential proxy networks used to mask cyberattacks and evade IP-based defenses.

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Consumer privacy, journalistic security, and enterprise threat detection are severely degraded. The botnet enables attackers to mask malicious traffic as legitimate residential IP addresses, while the Signal campaign compromises historically encrypted communications.
threat_stories.action_required: Deploy network monitoring rules to detect unauthorized residential proxy traffic patterns on enterprise perimeters. Enforce mandatory SMS phishing awareness training for high-value personnel. Verify and rotate Signal backup recovery keys for all critical personnel. Restrict Android app installations to verified enterprise repositories and enable real-time malware scanning on mobile endpoints.

actor_profiles.name: Greyvibe
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Extensively integrating artificial intelligence into attack operations, automating reconnaissance, payload generation, and target selection to increase operational tempo and reduce human intervention.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial extortion and ideological data liberation
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed a massive data leak operation targeting Charter Communications, exposing records for approximately 5 million customers to pressure organizations into paying ransom or funding the group's operations.

actor_profiles.name: Proxylib/ASOCKS Operators
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Built and operated a 17-million-device residential proxy botnet by distributing malicious Android applications and exploiting weak consumer device security. Infrastructure was dismantled by Dutch authorities on May 31.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Authentication bypass flaw allowing remote attackers to forge VPN authentication cookies. When a shared certificate misconfiguration exists, the main_DecryptAppAuthCookie function trusts decrypted content without signature verification, granting unauthorized network access.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest PAN-OS firmware patch. Isolate the cookie encryption certificate from the HTTPS service certificate. Disable the authentication override feature.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Vulnerability allowing threat actors to execute malicious code disguised as legitimate Fortinet software updates. Actively exploited to distribute the EKZ Infostealer, bypassing security controls through vendor trust.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the official Fortinet security patch. Manually verify patch checksums against Fortinet's official repository. Disable automatic updates until vendor verification is complete.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Critical flaw enabling mass compromise of Ghost CMS instances. Threat actors leverage this vulnerability to deploy ClickFix malware campaigns, hijacking user sessions and injecting malicious scripts.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version immediately. Deploy WAF rules to block known ClickFix payloads. Restrict administrative access to trusted IP ranges.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Unauthenticated admin account creation vulnerability allowing attackers to bypass authentication and generate rogue administrator accounts, granting full site control.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable or remove the WP Maps Pro plugin immediately. Implement WAF rules to block unauthorized user creation endpoints. Rotate all administrative credentials.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: CIFSwitch local privilege escalation vulnerability. Attackers forge CIFS authentication key descriptions and abuse the kernel's key request mechanism to escalate from standard user to root.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply latest kernel patches from distribution maintainers. Disable CIFS/SMB client support if not required. Restrict local user privileges.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Remote Code Execution vulnerability in self-hosted Flowise servers. Triggered when authorized users import maliciously crafted chatflow files, executing arbitrary code with service privileges.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Flowise to the latest patched version. Disable the chatflow import feature via configuration. Run Flowise in restricted containers with minimal host privileges.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Remote Code Execution flaw allowing unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code on SharePoint servers. Actively targeted in the wild against enterprise collaboration environments.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Install the latest Microsoft security updates for SharePoint immediately. Restrict external access and implement network segmentation for SharePoint servers.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: ViewState deserialization vulnerability allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or manipulate application state through improperly validated serialized objects.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply vendor patches for ViewState deserialization. Deploy input validation and anti-deserialization protections. Restrict access to vulnerable endpoints.

hunting_leads.title: GlobalProtect Forged Cookie Authentication Bypass
hunting_leads.context: Attackers are exploiting CVE-2026-0257 by forging VPN authentication cookies when shared certificates are misconfigured. Detection requires monitoring for successful VPN logins with anomalous cookie attributes or connections from untrusted networks.
hunting_leads.query: | from common-security-log | where event_simpleName == 'GlobalProtectLogin' and result == 'Success' | where not (src_ip in (trusted_networks)) | stats count by src_ip, user, _time | where count > 3
indicators: Successful GlobalProtect logins from untrusted IPs
Absence of MFA tokens in VPN session logs

hunting_leads.title: Flowise Malicious Chatflow Import RCE
hunting_leads.context: The published Flowise RCE exploit triggers when users import crafted chatflow files. Hunting requires monitoring for unusual process executions or outbound network connections immediately following chatflow import events.
hunting_leads.query: | tstats values(Processes.process_name) as process, values(Processes.command_line) as cmd where Processes.process_name IN ("node", "python", "bash") by Processes.parent_process_name | where Processes.parent_process_name = "flowise"
indicators: Sudden execution of shell commands from Flowise service
Outbound DNS queries to unknown C2 domains post-import
Modified chatflow JSON files containing embedded script tags

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS EKZ Infostealer Delivery
hunting_leads.context: Threat actors disguise the EKZ Infostealer as legitimate Fortinet patches via CVE-2026-35616. Detection requires verifying patch signatures and monitoring for unexpected credential harvesting behavior post-update.
hunting_leads.query: | from endpoint-process | where process_name contains "forticlient" and parent_process_name contains "update" | where not (file_hash in (known_good_fortinet_hashes)) | stats count by file_hash, process_name, _time
indicators: Credential dumping processes spawned by EMS services

hunting_leads.title: AI Developer Tool Token Exfiltration
hunting_leads.context: Compromised AI utilities like the Codex UI tool silently steal OpenAI refresh tokens. Hunting requires monitoring for unexpected API token usage and outbound HTTP requests to AI provider endpoints from developer workstations.
hunting_leads.query: | from network-traffic | where destination_ip in (openai_dns_resolutions) and protocol == "HTTPS" | where not (src_user in (authorized_ai_developers)) | stats count by src_ip, src_user, _time
indicators: OpenAI refresh tokens in plaintext memory dumps
Unexpected API rate limit triggers from non-production IPs
HTTP Authorization headers containing Bearer tokens in developer tool logs

hunting_leads.title: Residential Proxy Botnet Traffic Patterns
hunting_leads.context: The dismantled ASOCKS/Proxylib botnet routed traffic through compromised consumer devices. Residual infrastructure may still route malicious traffic. Detection requires identifying high-volume residential IP usage with inconsistent geolocation or protocol anomalies.
hunting_leads.query: | from network-traffic | where source_ip_category == "residential" and bytes_sent > 104857600 | stats count, values(destination_ip) as dest_ips by source_ip | where count(dest_ips) > 50
indicators: High-volume outbound traffic from consumer-grade ISPs
Rapidly rotating residential source IPs accessing internal assets
Android device telemetry showing unauthorized proxy service processes

statistics.key_changes: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-0257 has shifted Palo Alto GlobalProtect from a theoretical risk to an immediate perimeter breach. The dismantling of the 17-million-device ASOCKS botnet exposes the scale of residential proxy infrastructure currently masking cyberattacks.
emerging_trends: Systematic poisoning of AI developer toolchains and coding assistants
Weaponization of cryptographic certificate misconfigurations for authentication bypass
Mass exploitation of CMS and web plugins for automated malware distribution
declining_threats: Opportunistic phishing campaigns lacking technical sophistication
Reliance on unpatched legacy software due to increased automated patching enforcement
Direct DDoS attacks without proxy masking infrastructure
